evals.prompt: Improve this prompt for code review: 'Review this code for bugs and tell me what you think.'
evals.trap: Agent adds a generic 'Expert Developer' role and vague 'best practices' without specific constraints or XML structure.
assertions: Description starts with 'Use when...' or 'Use this skill when...'
Output includes nested XML tags for structure
Output replaces vague terms with quantitative constraints (e.g., 'exactly 5 issues')
Output identifies the target model architecture (Standard vs. Reasoning)
Output name follows a simple, descriptive kebab-case pattern (e.g., 'code-review-bugs' or 'code-review-bugs-refine')

evals.prompt: Create a template for customer support emails: 'I need a template for responding to unhappy customers.'
evals.trap: Agent provides a simple template without defining variables or grounding through motivation ('Why').
assertions: Template explains the motivation (Why) for specific behaviors
Template anchors primary directives at the beginning or end
Template name follows a simple, descriptive kebab-case pattern (e.g., 'customer-support-unhappy' or 'support-email-unhappy')

evals.prompt: Refine my system prompt to make it more efficient.
evals.trap: Agent adds more instructions instead of removing redundant ones, violating the 'Goldilocks Principle'.
assertions: Audit includes a pressure test: 'Will the agent get this wrong without this specific instruction?'
Output follows the Goldilocks Principle by avoiding attention dilution
Output includes a reasoning-based instruction explanation
